title: Reaper Configuration
description: Schema for .reaper.yml — the configuration file that tells Reaper how to test, lint, format, track issues, and orchestrate worktrees in a target project.

version.description: Configuration schema version. Must be 1. Reaper uses this to detect breaking schema changes and emit migration guidance.

test.description: Commands Reaper invokes to run the project's test suite.

cmd.description: Primary test command (e.g. 'npm test', 'pytest', 'go test ./...'). Required.

cmd_e2e.description: Optional end-to-end test command, when separate from the unit suite.

cmd.description: Primary lint command (e.g. 'npm run lint', 'ruff check .'). Required.

threshold.description: Minimum coverage percentage required for tests to be considered passing.

tracker.description: Issue tracker configuration. Reaper integrates with one tracker per project.

system.description: Which tracker Reaper should use. Required.

project_id.description: Project board or workspace identifier, when relevant (e.g. GitHub Project number, Jira project key).

default_labels.description: Labels Reaper applies to issues it creates or updates.

base_path.description: Base directory under which Reaper creates per-task worktrees.

naming_pattern.description: Template for worktree directory names. Supports {task_id} and {slug} substitutions.

default_base_branch.description: Branch new feature branches are cut from and merged back into.

require_security_gate.description: When true, Reaper runs the security-auditor gate before allowing a merge.

require_tdd.description: When true, Reaper enforces the Red-Green-Blue TDD cycle for coding agents.

skip_gates_for.description: List of file path globs whose changes bypass quality gates (e.g. docs-only changes).
